title: I Tried the Cheapest ($3,500) and Most Expensive ($120,000) FIFA 2026 Packages — the Difference Is Insane
excerpt: I went to two matches: one on a $3,500 standard ticket, one in a $120,000 Chairman’s Suite. I took notes on everything — the food, the queue, the bathroom, the view. You won’t believe what happened.
content:
Two matches. Same tournament. Different planets.

Match 1: Group stage at Lumen Field. Standard seat. $3,500.

Match 2: The Final at MetLife Stadium. Chairman’s Suite. $120,000.

I went to both. Here’s what I learned.

## The Arrival

$3,500: I parked in the general lot. Walked 22 minutes to the gate. Waited in line for 15 minutes while someone argued about a bag that was “technically within the size limit.”

$120,000: A driver met me at the car. Handed me a card. “Follow me, sir.” I was through a private entrance in 90 seconds. No bag check. No line.

## The Seat

$3,500: Row 34. Good view of the field. Could see most of the far touchline. Couldn’t see the near corner flag without leaning forward.

$120,000: The suite is at midfield. Glass front. Temperature-controlled. I stood up, walked three steps, and was looking straight down at the center line.

## The Food

$3,500: A hot dog under a heat lamp for God knows how long. $14. Bought it because I was hungry. Regretted it.

$120,000: Lobster thermidor at halftime. Served on actual plates. The chef came out and asked if everything was to my liking.

## The Bathroom

$3,500: Porta-potties. Enough said.

$120,000: Private. Marble countertops. Heated floors. A staff member handed me a hot towel on the way out.

## The Moment That Got Me

It wasn’t the lobster. It wasn’t the private entrance. It wasn’t even the heated floors.

It was the third goal of the Final. The stadium exploded — 82,500 people screaming at once. And from my suite, I heard it a split second before the crowd did. Because I was closer to the field than almost anyone.

That’s what $120,000 buys. Not the food. Not the butler. The moment.

But here’s the truth: the $3,500 ticket was fun. You’re at the World Cup. It’s impossible to have a bad time.

The $120,000 ticket? I’ll remember every second for the rest of my life.
